title: Shared Memory, Mutexes, Atomics, the Memory Model, and Race Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: Concurrent workers update shared counters, caches, and snapshots; plausible output hides races and broken publication. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a race-free shared-state component. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify conflicting memory operations and establish sequenced-before, synchronized-before, and happens-before evidence.
Protect one documented invariant with Mutex or RWMutex while minimizing lock scope and avoiding copy or reentrancy errors.
Use Once, OnceValue, Cond, and channel alternatives only when their wakeup, predicate-loop, and failure semantics fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Data Races Happens Before · changed case · Quiz Data Races Happens Before
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Workers concurrently publish a map-backed snapshot and increment two related counters. Output looks plausible in 4 runs. Establish happens-before edges, choose channel, mutex, or atomic ownership, and state what go test -race can and cannot prove.
